content.problemStatement:
Alice thought of a function FFF, which takes an arbitrary array of integers A=(A1,A2,…,AM)A = (A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_M)A=(A1​,A2​,…,AM​) as the only argument and is defined in the following way:


Consider all pairs of subarrays (Ai,Ai+1,…,Aj)(A_i, A_{i+1}, \ldots, A_j)(Ai​,Ai+1​,…,Aj​) and (Ak,Ak+1,…,Al)(A_k, A_{k+1}, \ldots, A_l)(Ak​,Ak+1​,…,Al​) such that:

1≤i≤j<k≤l≤M1 \le i \le j \lt k \le l \le M1≤i≤j<k≤l≤M
both subarrays are non-decreasing
Aj≤AkA_j \le A_kAj​≤Ak​


If there is no such pair of subarrays, F(A)=0F(A) = 0F(A)=0.
Otherwise, F(A)F(A)F(A) is the maximum of the expression (j−i+1)+(l−k+1)(j-i+1) + (l-k+1)(j−i+1)+(l−k+1) over all such pairs of subarrays.
In other words, you need to choose two non-overlapping non-empty subarrays of AAA and concatenate them in the same order; the resulting array should be non-decreasing and F(A)F(A)F(A) is its maximum possible length.

Bob challenged Alice to solve the following problem and she needs your help.

You are given a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN), rooted at the vertex 111. There is an integer written in each vertex; for each valid iii, let's denote the integer written in vertex iii by ViV_iVi​. You should choose one vertex ― let's denote it by vvv. Then, consider an array AAA containing values which are written in vertices on the path from the root to vvv (inclusive), in this order, and calculate F(A)F(A)F(A). Find the maximum possible value of F(A)F(A)F(A) which you can obtain this way.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers V1,V2,⋅,VNV_1, V_2, \cdot, V_NV1​,V2​,⋅,VN​.
Each of the following N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that vertices uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum of F(A)F(A)F(A).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We choose v=3v = 3v=3. Then, A(3)=(2,5)A(3) = (2, 5)A(3)=(2,5); its subarrays (2)(2)(2) and (5)(5)(5) satisfy all required conditions and the sum of their lengths is 222. There is no way to get a greater value of the function FFF.
